description: Project Foundation & Brand Design System - Set up the standalone Next.js marketing site with design tokens, shared layout components (header, footer, sections), and Vercel deployment pipeline

userStories.title: Next.js project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working Next.js project in /publicweb so that all subsequent pages have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 15+ with App Router and TypeScript strict mode initialized in publicweb/
Tailwind CSS v4 configured with custom theme tokens in tailwind.config.ts
shadcn/ui installed and configured (consistent with the main app's component library)
ESLint and Prettier configured with consistent rules
publicweb/ folder is self-contained with its own package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.ts
npm run dev starts the dev server on port 3001 (different from the main app)
All page content statically generated (SSG) via App Router — site must be fully functional with JavaScript disabled
Typecheck passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: No dependencies. This is the foundation PRD — all other PW PRDs depend on this.

userStories.title: Brand design tokens and theme
userStories.description: As a designer/developer, I want a defined set of brand tokens so that every page has a consistent, professional look.
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette defined in Tailwind config: primary (brand blue), secondary (accent), neutral grays, success/warning/error semantic colors
Typography scale defined: heading sizes (h1-h4), body, small, caption using Inter or Geist via next/font
Spacing scale consistent with Tailwind defaults
Border radius, shadow, and transition tokens defined in Tailwind config
Dark mode variant for all color tokens via CSS variables and next-themes
Dark/light mode toggle persisted in localStorage via next-themes
Font files self-hosted via next/font (no external Google Fonts requests) for performance and privacy
A /style-guide dev-only page showing all tokens rendered (colors, type scale, buttons, cards)
Visual style: clean, modern, professional — inspired by Linear, Vercel, or Stripe marketing sites
Color palette feels trustworthy and operational — blues with warm accents
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Design direction: generous whitespace, large headings, high-contrast text.

userStories.title: Page wrapper and section layout components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable page and section components so that all marketing pages have consistent spacing and structure.
acceptanceCriteria: PageWrapper component: sets max-width, horizontal padding, vertical spacing between sections
Section component: accepts variant prop (default, alternate-bg, dark, gradient) for visual rhythm
SectionHeader component: eyebrow text, heading, subheading — centered or left-aligned via prop
Container component: constrains content width (max-w-7xl)
Grid component: responsive grid with 2/3/4 column options via prop
All components responsive across mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints
All images use Next.js <Image> component with proper sizing and lazy loading
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 for design tokens. These components are reused by all subsequent PRDs.

userStories.title: Shared layout — Header with navigation
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a clear navigation header so that I can find any page on the site.
acceptanceCriteria: Sticky header with logo (left), nav links (center), and CTA button (right)
Nav links: Features, Pricing, Blog, Help, Contact
Start Free Trial primary CTA button in header linking to /signup
Mobile: hamburger menu with slide-out drawer containing all nav links and CTA
Active link highlighting based on current route
Header becomes slightly opaque/blurred on scroll (glassmorphism effect via backdrop-blur)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 and US-003. Nav links point to pages built in PRDs PW-02 through PW-07.

userStories.title: Shared layout — Footer
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a footer with links and company info so that I can navigate to secondary pages and find contact details.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-column footer layout: Logo+tagline+social links (LinkedIn, Facebook, X), Product links, Resources links, Company links, Legal links
Product column: Features, Pricing, Changelog
Resources column: Blog, Help Center, FAQ, API Docs
Company column: About, Contact, Careers
Legal column: Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy
Bottom bar with copyright notice and region selector placeholder (NZ/AU/UK/US flags)
Responsive: stacks to 2-column on tablet, single column on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 and US-003. Region selector fully implemented in PW-08.

userStories.title: Root layout with header, footer, and theme provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the root layout to include the header, footer, and theme provider so that all pages share the same shell.
acceptanceCriteria: Root layout (app/layout.tsx) wraps all pages with ThemeProvider (next-themes), header, and footer
Dark/light mode toggle button accessible from the header
Metadata defaults set: site title 'VanMan — Moving Company Software', description, OG image placeholder
All pages render within the shared header/footer shell
Site fully functional with JavaScript disabled (SSG/SSR for all content pages)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 and US-005. This wires up the shared layout for all pages across all PRDs.

userStories.title: Vercel deployment pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the public website to deploy automatically so that changes are live without manual steps.
acceptanceCriteria: vercel.json or Vercel project configured for the /publicweb directory (monorepo root directory override)
Production deploys on push to main branch
Preview deploys on pull requests
Environment variables placeholder documented in .env.example
Custom domain support ready (domain TBD — vanman.app or similar)
Build succeeds with no errors or warnings
Lighthouse performance score target: 95+, accessibility score target: 90+
Time to First Byte (TTFB) target: under 200ms
Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) target: under 2.5s
Build time target: under 60 seconds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006. This should be the final story — validates the full build works end-to-end.
